title: QA: BACKGROUND_SHELLS is process-global LazyLock singleton (DIP violation)
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
decoupling
## Location
src/tools/bash/mod.rs:271-272
## Violation
`BACKGROUND_SHELLS` is a process-wide `LazyLock<BackgroundShellManager>` singleton. Tests share state, multi-tenant deployments cannot isolate per-session shells, and the bash module cannot be unit-tested without spawning real processes. This is a textbook DIP violation: high-level callers (the tool dispatcher) depend on a concrete global instead of an injected `&dyn ShellManager`.

Quoted (mod.rs:270-272):
```
/// Global background shell manager
pub static BACKGROUND_SHELLS: std::sync::LazyLock<BackgroundShellManager> =
    std::sync::LazyLock::new(BackgroundShellManager::new);
```

The pinning tests acknowledge the problem ("Because the global `BACKGROUND_SHELLS` is shared across the test binary, this test might interact with others" — mod.rs:479-482).
## Mandated refactor
1. Introduce `pub trait ShellManager: Send + Sync { fn spawn(...); fn kill(...); fn list(...); fn get_output(...); }`.
2. `BackgroundShellManager` becomes one impl; tests get an in-memory impl.
3. Plumb an `Arc<dyn ShellManager>` through the tool dispatch context (Session or ToolContext struct) instead of `pub static`.
4. Eliminate the `pub static` entirely once injection is in place.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
